## What Changed

No existing files modified.

## Commands Run

```bash
mkdir -p scripts/benchmark/output/bench-claude-sonnet-4-6-06_design-1778967838
# Write tools used for design.md and this report
```

---

## Test Files and Totals

No test suite applicable for a design document output. Dispatch type `06_design` produces a markdown artifact, not executable code. No pytest run performed.

---

## Design Coverage

All five required sections delivered with concrete numbers:

| Section | Delivered |
|---|---|
| 1. Architecture | Component map, scaling units (pods × concurrency), communication rationale (Redis Streams vs NATS), sync/async boundary |
| 2. Data Flow | 11-step write log, state machine (7 states), CAS semantics, restart-durability table for 5 failure points |
| 3. Cost Routing | 6-stage algorithm (capability → health → budget → score → task-type override → approval gate), fallback chain, modeled cost mix ($4,873/day vs $32,250 all-Claude baseline) |
| 4. Failure Modes | 7 scenarios (provider rate-limit, Postgres failure, worker crash, cost ledger drift, Redis failure, budget exhaustion, approval backlog) — each with detection mechanism, recovery, blast radius |
| 5. SLOs | p50/p95/p99 per tier (Interactive/Standard/Batch), throughput floor (35 rps) and burst ceiling (58 rps), 5 error budgets, burn-rate alert config |

Architectural differentiator vs. the existing opus design: Redis Streams (lighter operational surface) instead of NATS JetStream; score-based single-pass routing instead of multi-step filter chain; Postgres as the sole authority for dispatch state (Redis holds only IDs in-transit, rebuildable from outbox table).

---

## Known Limitations

- No executable code produced; design cannot be integration-tested in this dispatch.
- Cost model uses assumed task-type distribution (25/15/15/10/10/5/10/10%) — actual distribution will shift the provider split.
- Provider API latency figures (p95 Claude ~3.7s, DeepSeek ~5.5s, Kimi ~15s for 1,500 output tokens) are estimates from public benchmarks at time of design; production calibration required.
- Redis async replica (not sync) means ≤1s of messages at risk on primary failure; full durability requires Postgres outbox poll recovery, which introduces up to 30s requeue latency for affected dispatches.

---

## Open Items

_(none — design document is complete as specified)_
